198	            find_cuda = re.compile(r"[\s](cuda\-[\d\.]{2,})$")
199	            possible_cudas = [find_cuda.search(x) for x in possible_cudas]
200	            possible_cudas = [x.group(1) for x in possible_cudas if x is not None]

202	            # Try linking cuda folder, or everything in local

205	            else:
206	                find_number = re.compile(r"([\d\.]{2,})")
207	                latest_cuda = np.argsort([float(find_number.search(x).group(1)) for x in possible_cudas])[::-1][0]
208	                latest_cuda = possible_cudas[latest_cuda]
209	                os.system(f"ldconfig /usr/local/{latest_cuda}")

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

272	        pass
273	    pass
274

283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")

290	        )

1322	    self,
1323	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
1324	    tokenizer            = None,
1325	    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
1326	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
1327	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
1328	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
1329	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
1330	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
1331	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
1332	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
1333	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
1334	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
1335	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
1336	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
1337	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
1338	):
1339	    """
1340	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1341	        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.
1342	
1343	        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
1344	        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
1345	        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
1346	        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
1347	    """
1348	    if tokenizer is None:
1349	        logger.warning_once(
1350	            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
1351	            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
1352	        )
1353	    pass
1354	
1355	    arguments = dict(locals())
1356	    arguments["model"] = self
1357	    del arguments["self"]
1358	    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1359	    for _ in range(3):
1360	        gc.collect()
1361	pass

1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",

1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",

2276	def save_to_gguf_generic(
2277	    model,
2278	    save_directory,
2279	    tokenizer,

2282	    repo_id = None,

2284	):
2285	    if token is None and repo_id is not None: token = get_token()
2286	    if repo_id is not None and token is None:
2287	        raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Please specify a token for uploading!")
2288	
2289	    if not os.path.exists(os.path.join("llama.cpp", "unsloth_convert_hf_to_gguf.py")):
2290	        install_llama_cpp(just_clone_repo = True)
2291	    pass
2292	
2293	    # Use old style quantization_method
2294	    new_quantization_methods = []
2295	    if quantization_method is not None:
2296	        # Convert quantization_method to list
2297	        if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
2298	        elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
2299	        elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
2300	        else:
2301	            raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
2302	        pass
2303	        for i, quant_method in enumerate(quantization_method):
2304	            quant_method = quant_method.lower()
2305	            if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = "f16"
2306	            elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
2307	            elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
2308	            elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"

2310	        pass
2311	    else:
2312	        new_quantization_methods.append(quantization_type.lower())
2313	    # Check if wrong method
2314	    for quant_method in new_quantization_methods:

2316	            error = f"Unsloth: Quant method = [{quant_method}] not supported. Choose from below:\n"
2317	            for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
2318	                error += f"[{key}] => {value}\n"
2319	            raise RuntimeError(error)
2320	        pass
2321	    pass
2322	
2323	    # Go through all types and save individually - somewhat inefficient

2330	        )

2341	            from huggingface_hub import HfApi

2346	                repo_type = "model",
2347	                allow_patterns = ["*.gguf"],
2348	            )
2349	        pass
2350	    pass
2351	    return metadata

2413	def unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(
2414	    self,
2415	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
2416	    tokenizer            = None,
2417	    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
2418	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
2419	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
2420	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
2421	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
2422	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
2423	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
2424	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
2425	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
2426	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
2427	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
2428	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
2429	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
2430	):
2431	    """
2432	        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
2433	        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.
2434	
2435	        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
2436	        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
2437	        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
2438	        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
2439	    """
2440	    if tokenizer is None:
2441	        logger.warning_once(
2442	            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
2443	            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
2444	        )
2445	    pass
2446	
2447	    arguments = dict(locals())
2448	    arguments["model"] = self
2449	    del arguments["self"]
2450	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)

65	def calculate_settings(n : int) -> (int, int,):
66	    BLOCK_SIZE : int = next_power_of_2(n)
67	    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:

69	                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
70	    num_warps : int = 4
71	    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
72	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
73	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
74	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X

275	if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu" and HAS_XPU_STREAM:
276	    @torch.inference_mode
277	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
278	        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
279	        if quant_state is None: return W
280	        if type(quant_state) is not list:
281	            # New quant_state as a class
282	            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
283	            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
284	            shape      = quant_state.shape
285	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
286	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
287	            offset     = quant_state.offset
288	            state2     = quant_state.state2
289	            absmax2    = state2.absmax
290	            code2      = state2.code
291	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize
292	        else:
293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
294	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass
298	        global XPU_STREAMS
299	        device = W.device
300	        device_index = device.index
301	        XPU_STREAM = XPU_STREAMS[device_index]
302	
303	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()
304	        # Create weight matrix
305	        if use_global_buffer:
306	
307	            # Use same buffers for faster inference
308	            size = shape[0]*shape[1]
309	            global WEIGHT_BUFFERS
310	            global ABSMAX_BUFFERS
311	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]
312	            ABSMAX_BUFFER = ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index]
313	            if WEIGHT_BUFFER is None:
314	                WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index] = WEIGHT_BUFFER = torch_empty(size, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
315	                ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index] = ABSMAX_BUFFER = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch.float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
316	
317	            if size > WEIGHT_BUFFER.numel(): WEIGHT_BUFFER.resize_(size)
318	            if n_elements_absmax > ABSMAX_BUFFER.numel(): ABSMAX_BUFFER.resize_(n_elements_absmax)
319	
320	            out = WEIGHT_BUFFER[:size].view(shape)
321	            out_absmax = ABSMAX_BUFFER[:n_elements_absmax]
322	        else:
323	            if out is None:
324	                out = torch_empty(shape, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
325	            else:
326	                assert(out.shape == shape)
327	                assert(out.dtype == dtype)
328	            out_absmax = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch_float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
329	        pass
330	
331	        # NF4 dequantization of statistics
332	        ptr_out_absmax = get_ptr(out_absmax)
333	        with torch_gpu_device(device):
334	            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32(
335	                get_ptr(code2), get_ptr(absmax), get_ptr(absmax2), ptr_out_absmax,
336	                ctypes_c_int(blocksize2), ctypes_c_int(n_elements_absmax), XPU_STREAM
337	            )
338	            out_absmax += offset
339	
340	            # Dequantize W
341	            fx = cdequantize_blockwise_fp16_nf4 if dtype == torch_float16 else \
342	                 cdequantize_blockwise_bf16_nf4
343	            fx(get_ptr(None), get_ptr(W), ptr_out_absmax, get_ptr(out),
344	               ctypes_c_int(blocksize), ctypes_c_int(out.numel()), XPU_STREAM,)
345	        pass
346	        # Careful returning transposed data
347	        is_transposed = (True if W.shape[0] == 1 else False)
348	        return out.t() if is_transposed else out
349	    pass
350	# NVIDIA GPU Default Logic
351	elif DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip") and HAS_CUDA_STREAM:
352	    @torch.inference_mode
353	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
354	        if quant_state is None: return W
355	        if type(quant_state) is not list:
356	            # New quant_state as a class
357	            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
358	            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
359	            shape      = quant_state.shape
360	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
361	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
362	            offset     = quant_state.offset
363	            state2     = quant_state.state2
364	            absmax2    = state2.absmax
365	            code2      = state2.code
366	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize
367	        else:
368	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
369	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
370	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
371	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
372	        pass
373	        global CUDA_STREAMS
374	        device = W.device
375	        device_index = device.index
376	        CUDA_STREAM = CUDA_STREAMS[device_index]
377	
378	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()
379	
380	        # Create weight matrix
381	        if use_global_buffer:
382	
383	            # Use same buffers for faster inference
384	            size = shape[0]*shape[1]
385	            global WEIGHT_BUFFERS
386	            global ABSMAX_BUFFERS
387	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]

107	class FastLanguageModel(FastLlamaModel):
108	    @staticmethod
109	    def from_pretrained(
110	        model_name                 = "unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
111	        max_seq_length             = 2048,
112	        dtype                      = None,
113	        load_in_4bit               = True,  # 4bit QLoRA
114	        load_in_8bit               = False, # 8bit  LoRA
115	        load_in_16bit              = False, # 16bit LoRA
116	        full_finetuning            = False,
117	        token                      = None,
118	        device_map                 = "sequential",
119	        rope_scaling               = None,
120	        fix_tokenizer              = True,
121	        trust_remote_code          = False,
122	        use_gradient_checkpointing = "unsloth",
123	        resize_model_vocab         = None,
124	        revision                   = None,
125	        use_exact_model_name       = False,
126	        offload_embedding          = False,
127	
128	        fast_inference             = False, # uses vLLM
129	        gpu_memory_utilization     = 0.5,
130	        float8_kv_cache            = False,
131	        random_state               = 3407,
132	        max_lora_rank              = 64,
133	        disable_log_stats          = True,
134	        qat_scheme                 = None,
135	        *args, **kwargs,
136	    ):
137	        # Login to allow private models
138	        if token is None: token = get_token()
139	        if token is not None:
140	            try:
141	                from huggingface_hub import login
142	                login(token = token)
143	            except:
144	                pass
145	        if load_in_8bit or full_finetuning or qat_scheme is not None:
146	            return FastModel.from_pretrained(
147	                model_name                 = model_name,
148	                max_seq_length             = max_seq_length,
149	                dtype                      = dtype,
150	                load_in_4bit               = load_in_4bit,
151	                load_in_8bit               = load_in_8bit,
152	                load_in_16bit              = load_in_16bit,
153	                full_finetuning            = full_finetuning,
154	                token                      = token,
155	                device_map                 = device_map,
156	                rope_scaling               = rope_scaling, # [TODO] No effect
157	                fix_tokenizer              = fix_tokenizer, # [TODO] No effect
158	                trust_remote_code          = trust_remote_code,
159	                use_gradient_checkpointing = use_gradient_checkpointing,
160	                resize_model_vocab         = resize_model_vocab, # [TODO] No effect
161	                revision                   = revision,
162	                return_logits              = False, # Return logits
163	                fullgraph                  = True, # No graph breaks
164	                use_exact_model_name       = use_exact_model_name,
165	                offload_embedding          = offload_embedding,
166	
167	                # Pass vLLM/inference parameters
168	                fast_inference             = fast_inference,
169	                gpu_memory_utilization     = gpu_memory_utilization,
170	                float8_kv_cache            = float8_kv_cache,
171	                random_state               = random_state,
172	                max_lora_rank              = max_lora_rank,
173	                disable_log_stats          = disable_log_stats,
174	
175	                qat_scheme                 = qat_scheme,
176	                *args, **kwargs,
177	            )
178	        pass
179	
180	        if token is None: token = get_token()
181	        if isinstance(dtype, str) and dtype in ["float16", "bfloat16"]:
182	            dtype = getattr(torch, dtype)
183	        assert (dtype is None or dtype == torch.float16 or dtype == torch.bfloat16
184	                or dtype == torch.float32)
185	
186	        if fast_inference:
187	            if importlib.util.find_spec("vllm") is None:
188	                raise ImportError(
189	                    "Unsloth: Please install vLLM before enabling `fast_inference`!\n"\
190	                    "You can do this in a terminal via `pip install vllm`"
191	                )
192	            pass
193	        pass
194	
195	        old_model_name = model_name
196	        if not use_exact_model_name:
197	            model_name = get_model_name(model_name, load_in_4bit)
198	
199	        if USE_MODELSCOPE and not os.path.exists(model_name):
200	            from modelscope import snapshot_download
201	            model_name = snapshot_download(model_name)
202	        pass
203	
204	        # First check if it's a normal model via AutoConfig
205	        from huggingface_hub.utils import disable_progress_bars, enable_progress_bars, are_progress_bars_disabled
206	        was_disabled = are_progress_bars_disabled()
207	        disable_progress_bars()
208	
209	        autoconfig_error = None
210	        peft_error = None
211	        model_config = None
212	        peft_config = None
213	        try:
214	            model_config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(
215	                model_name,
216	                token = token,
217	                revision = revision,
218	                trust_remote_code = trust_remote_code,
219	            )
220	            is_model = True
221	        except Exception as error:
222	            autoconfig_error = str(error)
223	            is_model = False
224	        try:
225	            peft_config = PeftConfig.from_pretrained(
226	                model_name,
227	                token = token,
228	                revision = revision,
229	                trust_remote_code = trust_remote_code,
230	            )
231	            is_peft = True
232	        except Exception as error:
233	            peft_error = str(error)
234	            is_peft = False
235	        pass
236	
237	        # Old transformers versions check
238	        both_exist = (is_model and is_peft) and not SUPPORTS_LLAMA32
239	
240	        # Error out if both LoRA and normal model config exists.
241	        if both_exist:
242	            raise RuntimeError(
243	                "Unsloth: Your repo has a LoRA adapter and a base model.\n"\
244	                "You have 2 files `config.json` and `adapter_config.json`.\n"\
245	                "We must only allow one config file.\n"\
246	                "Please separate the LoRA and base models to 2 repos."
247	            )
248	        model_types = get_transformers_model_type(
249	            peft_config if peft_config is not None else model_config
250	        )
251	        if len(model_types) == 1:
252	            model_type = model_types[0]
253	        else:
254	            # Leave as tuple if more than one arch
255	            model_type = model_types
256	
257	        # New transformers need to check manually.
258	        if SUPPORTS_LLAMA32:
259	            # Check if folder exists locally
260	            if os.path.isdir(model_name):
261	                exist_adapter_config = os.path.exists(os.path.join(model_name, "adapter_config.json"))
262	                exist_config         = os.path.exists(os.path.join(model_name, "config.json"))
263	                both_exist = exist_adapter_config and exist_config
264	            else:
265	                # Because HfFileSystem assumes linux paths, we need to set the path with forward slashes, even on Windows.

2587	    """

1951	def unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(
1952	    self,
1953	    repo_id              : str,
1954	    tokenizer            = None,
1955	    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
1956	    first_conversion     : str = None,
1957	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
1958	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
1959	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
1960	    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
1961	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
1962	    create_pr            : bool = False,
1963	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
1964	    revision             : str = None,
1965	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
1966	    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
1967	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
1968	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
1969	):
1970	    """
1971	        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1972	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1973	
1974	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1975	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1976	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1977	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1978	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1979	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1980	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1981	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1982	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1983	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1984	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1985	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1986	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1987	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1988	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1989	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1990	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1991	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1992	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1993	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1994	    """
1995	    if tokenizer is None:
1996	        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")
1997	
1998	    arguments = dict(locals())
1999	    arguments["model"]          = self
2000	    arguments["tokenizer"]      = tokenizer
2001	    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id
2002	    arguments["push_to_hub"]    = False # We save ourselves
2003	    arguments["save_method"]   = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
2004	    del arguments["self"]
2005	    del arguments["repo_id"]
2006	    del arguments["quantization_method"]
2007	    del arguments["first_conversion"]
2008	
2009	    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
2010	    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)
2011	
2012	    # Non blocking install GGUF first
2013	    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
2014	
2015	        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
2016	            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
2017	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
2018	            python_install.wait()
2019	            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
2020	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
2021	            makefile = None
2022	        else:
2023	            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()

2025	            git_clone.wait()
2026	            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
2027	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
2028	            python_install.wait()
2029	        pass
2030	    else:
2031	        try:
2032	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
2033	            makefile = None
2034	        except:
2035	            # Retry by recloning llama.cpp

2038	                python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])

1618	def create_ollama_modelfile(tokenizer, gguf_location):
1619	    """
1620	        Creates an Ollama Modelfile.
1621	        Use ollama.create(model = "new_ollama_model", modelfile = modelfile)
1622	    """
1623	    modelfile = getattr(tokenizer, "_ollama_modelfile", None)
1624	    if modelfile is None: return None
1625	
1626	    FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER = "⚫@✅#🦥__FILE_LOCATION__⚡@🦥#⛵"
1627	    EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER     = "⚫@✅#🦥__EOS_TOKEN__⚡@🦥#⛵"
1628	    LEFT_BRACKET_REPLACER  = "⚫@✅#🦥"
1629	    RIGHT_BRACKET_REPLACER = "⚡@🦥#⛵"
1630	
1631	    # Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1087
1632	    # We must convert all {'s and }'s but keep {__FILE_LOCATION__} intact
1633	    modelfile = modelfile\
1634	        .replace("{__FILE_LOCATION__}", FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER)\
1635	        .replace("{__EOS_TOKEN__}",     EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER)\
1636	        .replace("{", LEFT_BRACKET_REPLACER)\
1637	        .replace("}", RIGHT_BRACKET_REPLACER)
1638	
1639	    # Revert {__FILE_LOCATION__} back
1640	    modelfile = modelfile\
1641	        .replace(FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER, "{__FILE_LOCATION__}")\
1642	        .replace(EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER,     "{__EOS_TOKEN__}")
1643	
1644	    if "__EOS_TOKEN__" in modelfile:
1645	        modelfile = modelfile.format(
1646	            __FILE_LOCATION__  = gguf_location,
1647	            __EOS_TOKEN__      = tokenizer.eos_token,
1648	        )
1649	    else:
1650	        modelfile = modelfile.format(
1651	            __FILE_LOCATION__  = gguf_location,
1652	        )
1653	    pass
1654	
1655	    modelfile = modelfile\
1656	        .replace("⚫@✅#🦥", "{")\
1657	        .replace("⚡@🦥#⛵", "}")\
1658	        .rstrip()
1659	
1660	    return modelfile
1661	pass

1663	def create_ollama_model(
1664	    username: str,
1665	    model_name: str,
1666	    tag: str,
1667	    modelfile_path: str
1668	):
1669	    try:
1670	        init_check = subprocess.run(

1672	        )
1673	        if init_check.returncode == 0:
1674	            print(init_check.stdout.strip())
1675	        else:
1676	            print("Ollama Server is not Running")
1677	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
1678	        return "Ollama Request Timeout"
1679	
1680	    process = subprocess.Popen(
1681	            ['ollama', 'create', f'{username}/{model_name}:{tag}', '-f', f'{modelfile_path}'],
1682	        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
1683	        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
1684	        text=True,
1685	        bufsize=1,
1686	        universal_newlines=True
1687	    )
1688	
1689	    for line in iter(process.stdout.readline, ''):
1690	        print(line, end='')
1691	        sys.stdout.flush()
1692	
1693	    return_code = process.wait()
1694	
1695	    if return_code != 0:
1696	        print(f"\nMODEL CREATED FAILED WITH RETURN CODE {return_code}")
1697	    else:
1698	        print("\nMODEL CREATED SUCCESSFULLY")
1699	pass

513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]

content.text:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile the full report.

---

Here is a thorough analysis of the unsloth codebase.

---

## 1. Overall Directory Structure

```
unsloth/                        # Main package
├── __init__.py                 # Top-level public API; imports from models, save, chat_templates, tokenizer_utils, trainer
├── _auto_install.py
├── chat_templates.py
├── import_fixes.py
├── save.py                     # ALL save/export logic lives here
├── tokenizer_utils.py
├── trainer.py
│
├── kernels/                    # Custom Triton CUDA kernels
│   ├── __init__.py             # Re-exports get_lora_parameters, fast_dequantize, etc.
│   ├── fast_lora.py            # LoRA kernel implementations and LoRA param accessors
│   ├── utils.py                # get_lora_parameters, get_lora_parameters_bias, fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE
│   ├── cross_entropy_loss.py
│   ├── rms_layernorm.py
│   ├── layernorm.py
│   ├── rope_embedding.py
│   ├── swiglu.py
│   ├── geglu.py
│   └── moe/                   # MoE fused kernels
│
├── models/                    # Model patching and loading
│   ├── __init__.py            # Exports FastLlamaModel, FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, FastModel, etc.
│   ├── _utils.py              # Shared utilities, version: "2025.9.9"
│   ├── llama.py               # FastLlamaModel (base for most models)
│   ├── loader.py              # FastLanguageModel, FastModel, FastVisionModel, FastTextModel
│   ├── vision.py              # FastBaseModel (used by vision path)
│   ├── mistral.py, qwen2.py, qwen3.py, qwen3_moe.py, gemma.py, gemma2.py
│   ├── cohere.py, granite.py, falcon_h1.py, llama4.py
│   ├── dpo.py, rl.py, rl_replacements.py
│   ├── loader_utils.py
│   └── mapper.py
│
├── dataprep/
├── registry/                  # Model registry (llama, gemma, qwen, mistral, phi, deepseek)
└── utils/
    ├── __init__.py
    └── hf_hub.py

tests/
├── saving/                    # Extensive saving tests
│   ├── language_models/
│   ├── vision_models/
│   ├── text_to_speech_models/
│   └── non_peft/
└── qlora/
```

---

## 2. Save/Export Methods — All in `/unsloth/save.py`

The `__all__` in `save.py` (line 48-54) exports:
- `print_quantization_methods`
- `unsloth_save_model`
- `save_to_gguf`
- `patch_saving_functions`
- `create_huggingface_repo`

### Core internal functions:

**`_merge_lora(layer, name)`** (line 159)
The key merge function. Dequantizes 4-bit weights via `fast_dequantize`, then applies `W.addmm_(A.t(), B.t(), alpha=s)` to fuse LoRA into the base weight.

**`unsloth_save_model(model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method, push_to_hub, ..., maximum_memory_usage)`** (line 205)
The master save function. `save_method` can be `"lora"`, `"merged_16bit"`, or `"merged_4bit"`. For `merged_16bit`, it iterates through `internal_model.model.layers`, calls `_merge_lora` for each projection layer, and builds a state dict. Decorated with `@torch.inference_mode`.

**`save_to_gguf(model_type, model_dtype, is_sentencepiece, model_directory, quantization_method, first_conversion, _run_installer)`** (line 952)
Shells out to `llama.cpp` (`llama-quantize`, `convert-hf-to-gguf.py`). Installs llama.cpp from source if not present. Supports a wide range of GGUF quantization types (q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q8_0, etc.).

**`save_to_gguf_generic(model, save_directory, tokenizer, quantization_method, quantization_type, repo_id, token)`** (line 2276)
Newer path used by vision models. Delegates to `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp.convert_to_gguf`.

**`unsloth_generic_save(model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method, ...)`** (line 2356)
Delegates to `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils.merge_and_overwrite_lora`.

### Methods patched onto model instances (by `patch_saving_functions`):

These are all dynamically bound via `types.MethodType` in `patch_saving_functions` (line 2569):

| Method on model | Underlying function | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `model.save_pretrained_merged(...)` | `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged` | Save merged 16bit/4bit or LoRA |
| `model.push_to_hub_merged(...)` | `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged` | Push merged to HF Hub |
| `model.save_pretrained_gguf(...)` | `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf` | Save as GGUF (llama.cpp format) |
| `model.push_to_hub_gguf(...)` | `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf` | Push GGUF to HF Hub |
| `model.save_pretrained_torchao(...)` | `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` | Save with TorchAO quantization |
| `model.save_pretrained_ggml(...)` | `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally` | Convert LoRA to GGML format locally |
| `model.push_to_hub_ggml(...)` | `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub` | Convert LoRA to GGML and push |
| `model.push_to_hub(...)` | `unsloth_push_to_hub` (dynamically exec'd) | Wraps original with unsloth tags |

## 3. LoRA A/B Weight Storage and Access

### Where the weights live (in PEFT's layer objects)

LoRA weights are stored in standard PEFT `Linear`/`Linear4bit` layer attributes:
- **`proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight`** — the A matrix (down-projection), shape `[rank, in_features]`
- **`proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight`** — the B matrix (up-projection), shape `[out_features, rank]`
- **`proj.scaling[adapter_name]`** — the scalar `lora_alpha / rank`
- **`proj.base_layer.weight`** — the frozen base weight (may be 4-bit quantized)

### The accessor functions in `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`

**`get_lora_parameters(proj)`** (line 190) — returns a 5-tuple:
```python
(W, QUANT_STATE(W), A, B, scaling)
# W = base_layer.weight  (possibly NF4 quantized)
# A = proj.lora_A[adapter].weight
# B = proj.lora_B[adapter].weight
# scaling = proj.scaling[adapter]  (= lora_alpha/r)
```
Handles disabled adapters (returns `None` for A, B, scaling), supports fake-quantization for QAT.

**`get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)`** (line 235) — same as above plus `base_layer.bias` as 6th return value.

**`QUANT_STATE(W)`** (line 188) — `getattr(W, "quant_state", None)` — extracts the bitsandbytes NF4 quantization state.

### How A/B are accessed during merge (in `_merge_lora`):

```python
# In save.py line 159-187:
W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)          # dequantize from NF4 -> float
W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
if A is not None:
    W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    # i.e., W += s * A^T @ B^T  (which is the LoRA update W += s * B @ A)
W = W.t().to(dtype)
```

### How A/B are used during training/inference (in `fast_lora.py`):

The Triton-fused kernels (`LoRA_MLP`, `LoRA_QKV`, `LoRA_W`) take `(W, W_quant, A, B, scaling)` as direct parameters, obtained via `get_lora_parameters(proj)` at the start of each forward pass.

In `fast_lora_forward` (line 470), the standard PEFT path accesses them as:
```python
lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
```

- **`unsloth/save.py`** — contains all saving logic. Not a class; all functions are module-level.
- **`unsloth_zoo.saving_utils.merge_and_overwrite_lora`** — external dependency used by the generic save path.
- **`unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp.convert_to_gguf`** — external dependency for the generic GGUF path.
- **`peft.PeftModelForCausalLM`** / **`peft.PeftModelForSequenceClassification`** — the PEFT model wrappers that hold `lora_A`, `lora_B`, `scaling`.
- **`bitsandbytes.nn.Linear4bit`** (`Bnb_Linear4bit`) — base quantized layer.
- **`peft.tuners.lora.Linear4bit`** (`Peft_Linear4bit`) and **`peft.tuners.lora.Linear`** (`Peft_Linear`) — the PEFT LoRA-wrapped layers that contain `lora_A`, `lora_B`.

import torch
import os
import shutil

from huggingface_hub import HfApi

    from huggingface_hub import get_token

    try:
        from huggingface_hub.utils import get_token

        # For older versions of huggingface_hub
        from huggingface_hub.utils._token import get_token
    pass
pass

    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",

]

# enabling it will require much more work, so we have to prioritize. Please understand!
# We do have a beta version, which you can contact us about!

# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1266
os.environ["PROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION"] = "python"

# [TODO] Check why some GPUs don't work

#    "pinned_num_register_threads:8"

# Log Unsloth is being used
os.environ["UNSLOTH_IS_PRESENT"] = "1"

# Try importing PyTorch and check version
try:
    import torch
except ModuleNotFoundError:
    raise ImportError(
        "Unsloth: Pytorch is not installed. Go to https://pytorch.org/.\n"\
        "We have some installation instructions on our Github page."
    )
except Exception as exception:
    raise exception
pass

@functools.cache
def is_hip():
    return bool(getattr(getattr(torch, "version", None), "hip", None))
pass

@functools.cache
def get_device_type():
    if hasattr(torch, "cuda") and torch.cuda.is_available():
        if is_hip():
            return "hip"
        return "cuda"
    elif hasattr(torch, "xpu") and torch.xpu.is_available():
        return "xpu"
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth currently only works on NVIDIA GPUs and Intel GPUs.")
pass
DEVICE_TYPE : str = get_device_type()

@functools.cache
def get_device_count():
    if DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip"):
        return torch.cuda.device_count()
    elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
        return torch.xpu.device_count()
    else:
        return 1
pass

DEVICE_COUNT : int = get_device_count()

# Reduce VRAM usage by reducing fragmentation
# And optimize pinning of memory
# TODO(billishyahao): need to add hip related optimization...
if (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="0"):
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = \
        "expandable_segments:True,"\
        "roundup_power2_divisions:[32:256,64:128,256:64,>:32]"
elif (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="1") and \
    ("expandable_segments:True" in os.environ.get("PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF", "")):
    warnings.warn(
        "Unsloth: `UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY` is on, but requires `expandable_segments` to be off.\n"\
        "We will remove `expandable_segments`.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = re.sub(
        r"expandable\_segments\:True\,?",
        "",
        os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"],
    )
pass
# We support Pytorch 2
# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/38
torch_version = str(re.match(r"[0-9\.]{3,}", str(torch.__version__)).group(0)).split(".")
major_torch, minor_torch = torch_version[0], torch_version[1]
major_torch, minor_torch = int(major_torch), int(minor_torch)
if (major_torch < 2):
    raise ImportError("Unsloth only supports Pytorch 2 for now. Please update your Pytorch to 2.1.\n"\
                      "We have some installation instructions on our Github page.")
elif (major_torch == 2) and (minor_torch < 2):
    # Disable expandable_segments
    del os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"]
pass

# CCE fails on Torch 2.8 and above
# OutOfResources: out of resource: shared memory, Required: 98304, Hardware limit: 65536. Reducing block sizes or `num_stages`
if (major_torch >= 2 and minor_torch >= 8) or (major_torch > 2):
    os.environ["UNSLOTH_ENABLE_CCE"] = "0"
pass

    old_is_bf16_supported = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported
    if "including_emulation" in str(inspect.signature(old_is_bf16_supported)):
        def is_bf16_supported(including_emulation = False):

        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported

            # Sometimes bitsandbytes cannot be linked properly in Runpod for example
            possible_cudas = subprocess.check_output(["ls", "-al", "/usr/local"]).decode("utf-8").split("\n")
            find_cuda = re.compile(r"[\s](cuda\-[\d\.]{2,})$")
            possible_cudas = [find_cuda.search(x) for x in possible_cudas]
            possible_cudas = [x.group(1) for x in possible_cudas if x is not None]

            # Try linking cuda folder, or everything in local
            if len(possible_cudas) == 0:

            else:
                find_number = re.compile(r"([\d\.]{2,})")
                latest_cuda = np.argsort([float(find_number.search(x).group(1)) for x in possible_cudas])[::-1][0]

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

@torch.inference_mode
def export_lora_to_onnx(
    model,
    output_dir    : str,
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> None:
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.

    For every LoRA-augmented linear layer in *model* this function builds a
    small, self-contained ONNX graph that computes the *adapter delta*:

        lora_delta = scaling * ((x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T)

    where
        - ``lora_A`` has shape ``[rank, in_features]``
        - ``lora_B`` has shape ``[out_features, rank]``
        - ``scaling`` equals ``alpha / rank``

    Each layer is written as a separate ``<module_name>.onnx`` file inside
    *output_dir*.  A ``lora_onnx_metadata.json`` file is also written with
    the rank, alpha, scaling factor, feature dimensions, and the set of
    target modules that were exported.

    Parameters
    ----------
    model : PeftModel / FastLanguageModel
        The (P)EFT model whose active LoRA adapters should be exported.
    output_dir : str
        Directory to write ``.onnx`` files and metadata into.
        Created automatically if it does not exist.
    opset_version : int, optional
        ONNX opset version to use (default ``17``).

    Returns
    -------
    None

    Raises
    ------
    ImportError
        If the ``onnx`` package is not installed.

    Examples
    --------
    >>> from unsloth import FastLanguageModel
    >>> model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained("my-lora-model")
    >>> from unsloth.save import export_lora_to_onnx
    >>> export_lora_to_onnx(model, output_dir="./lora_onnx", opset_version=17)
    """
    import json
    import numpy as np

    try:
        import onnx
        from onnx import numpy_helper, TensorProto, helper as onnx_helper
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: The `onnx` package is required for ONNX export.\n"
            "Install it with:  pip install onnx"
        )
    pass

    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    metadata : dict = {
        "format"        : "unsloth_lora_onnx",
        "opset_version" : opset_version,
        "adapters"      : [],
    }
    target_modules : set = set()
    exported_count : int = 0

    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
        # Only process PEFT LoRA wrapper layers that carry lora_A / lora_B
        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
            continue

        # Skip layers where adapters are disabled or already merged into base
        if getattr(module, "disable_adapters", False) or getattr(module, "merged", False):
            continue

        # Resolve the active adapter name (supports both old and new PEFT APIs)
        active = getattr(module, "active_adapters", None)
        if active is None:
            active = getattr(module, "active_adapter", ("default",))
        adapter_name: str = active[0] if isinstance(active, (list, tuple)) else active

        if adapter_name not in module.lora_A:
            continue  # adapter weights not present for this layer

        lora_A_weight = module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight  # [rank, in_features]
        lora_B_weight = module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight  # [out_features, rank]
        scaling       = float(module.scaling[adapter_name])

        rank        : int = lora_A_weight.shape[0]
        in_features : int = lora_A_weight.shape[1]
        out_features: int = lora_B_weight.shape[0]

        # Recover alpha; PEFT stores it in lora_alpha[adapter_name]
        lora_alpha_map = getattr(module, "lora_alpha", {})
        alpha = float(lora_alpha_map.get(adapter_name, scaling * rank))

        # Convert to float32 numpy arrays for ONNX
        A_T = lora_A_weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy().T  # [in_features, rank]
        B_T = lora_B_weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy().T  # [rank, out_features]

        # ------------------------------------------------------------------
        # Build the ONNX graph:

        #   input  (float32, [batch?, seq?, in_features])

        init_scale = numpy_helper.from_array(
            np.array([scaling], dtype=np.float32), name="scaling"

            "MatMul",
            inputs  = ["input", "lora_A_T"],
            outputs = ["h"],
            name    = "MatMul_lora_A",
        )
        node_matmul_b = onnx_helper.make_node(
            "MatMul",
            inputs  = ["h", "lora_B_T"],
            outputs = ["lora_delta_unscaled"],
            name    = "MatMul_lora_B",
        )
        node_scale = onnx_helper.make_node(
            "Mul",
            inputs  = ["lora_delta_unscaled", "scaling"],
            outputs = ["lora_delta"],
            name    = "Mul_scaling",
        )

        graph = onnx_helper.make_graph(

            "Unsloth LoRA adapter delta for layer '{}' "
            "(rank={}, alpha={:.4g}, scaling={:.6g})".format(

        onnx.checker.check_model(model_proto)

        safe_name  = module_name.replace(".", "_").replace("/", "_")
        onnx_file  = "{}.onnx".format(safe_name)
        onnx_path  = os.path.join(output_dir, onnx_file)
        onnx.save(model_proto, onnx_path)

        # Track which weight names were adapted (e.g. "q_proj", "v_proj")
        target_modules.add(module_name.split(".")[-1])

        metadata["adapters"].append({
            "module_name" : module_name,
            "onnx_file"   : onnx_file,
            "adapter_name": adapter_name,
            "rank"        : rank,
            "alpha"       : alpha,
            "scaling"     : scaling,
            "in_features" : in_features,
            "out_features": out_features,
        })
        exported_count += 1
    pass  # end of named_modules loop

    if exported_count == 0:
        import warnings
        warnings.warn(
            "Unsloth: No active LoRA adapter layers were found in the model. "
            "Nothing was exported."
        )
    pass

    metadata["target_modules"] = sorted(target_modules)

    meta_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_onnx_metadata.json")
    with open(meta_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
        json.dump(metadata, fh, indent=2)

    print(
        "Unsloth: Exported {} LoRA adapter(s) to ONNX format in '{}'.".format(
            exported_count, output_dir
        )
    )
    if target_modules:
        print("         Target modules : {}".format(sorted(target_modules)))
    print("         Metadata saved : {}".format(meta_path))

def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir    : str,
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> None:
    """
    Export this model's LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.

    This is a convenience wrapper around :func:`export_lora_to_onnx` that
    is bound directly to the model object by
    :func:`patch_saving_functions`.  It mirrors the ``save_pretrained_*``
    naming convention used by the other Unsloth export methods.

    For every LoRA-augmented linear layer a self-contained ONNX graph is
    written to *output_dir* that computes the adapter delta::

        lora_delta = scaling * ((x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T)

    A ``lora_onnx_metadata.json`` file with rank, alpha, scaling, feature
    dimensions, and the list of target modules is also written.

    Parameters
    ----------
    output_dir : str
        Directory to write ``.onnx`` files and metadata into.
        Created automatically if it does not exist.
    opset_version : int, optional
        ONNX opset version (default ``17``).

    Examples
    --------
    >>> model.save_pretrained_onnx("./lora_onnx_export")
    >>> model.save_pretrained_onnx("./lora_onnx_export", opset_version=18)
    """
    export_lora_to_onnx(self, output_dir=output_dir, opset_version=opset_version)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect

def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
            model.save_pretrained_onnx   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,                  model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
